one_time_description: Share of loss from Government Business Enterprise (Nigig Power Corporation) of $23,906,232 and share of loss from Government Business Partnerships of $161,611 recorded under modified equity method
economic_notes: Henvey Inlet Wind LP contributed $4,355,687 in revenue; Pattern Renewable Energy contributed $1,897,175 in revenue. First Nation holds 33.33% equity in Shwe Miikaan Corp/LP and 50% equity in HIW Property Holdings LP/GP. Post year-end, issued $117,052,000 FNFA promissory note to refinance NPC debt at 2.15% per annum maturing 2039. COVID-19 declared pandemic March 11, 2020 with unknown financial impact. Contingent liability of $173,197,700 legal claim against First Nation and NPC.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020 (not 2024). Two consolidated subsidiaries: Henvey Inlet First Nation Key Harbour Settlement Trust and Pickerel Contracting Limited (PCL consolidated this year as it no longer meets GBE definition). One GBE: Nigig Power Corporation (NPC), accounted for on modified equity basis — NPC investment written down to $1 due to massive losses; NPC net liabilities of $8.8M. Two government business partnerships (SMLP and HIWP) also on modified equity. Large non-cash losses of $24M from GBE/GBP equity method distort annual surplus. Post year-end $117M FNFA promissory note issued. Contingent legal claim of $173.2M. Revenue figure of $14,315,067 per statement; Note 10 shows $14,178,871 (difference of $136,196 related to PCL consolidation adjustment). Housing expenses estimated from Band Housing $149,402 + Social Housing $212,576 = $361,978. total_government_transfers estimated as Federal $3,003,691 + Provincial $993,180 + Kinoomaadziwin Education Body $1,246,233 + Grants and other subsidies $322,122 = $5,565,226 (rounded to $5,565,096 per available data). net_financial_assets not directly stated on face of statements provided.
